GTA VI features two playable protagonists for the first time in series history — Lucia and Jason.
The game returns to Vice City, Rockstar's vibrant take on Miami and the wider state of Leonida.
Lucia is the first female lead protagonist in a mainline Grand Theft Auto game.
The reveal trailer became the most-viewed video-game trailer in 24 hours, racking up over 90 million views in a single day.
The world is said to be the largest and most detailed Rockstar has ever created.
NPCs are powered by a new behavior system, reacting more dynamically to the player than ever before.
Dynamic weather brings rolling storms, tropical heat and atmospheric fog to the streets of Vice City.
Wildlife roams the swamps and beaches of Leonida — from alligators to flocks of flamingos.
The trailer's signature track, 'Love Is a Long Road' by Tom Petty, set the tone for the whole reveal.
Vehicles, animations and physics have all been rebuilt to push current-gen consoles to their limit.
The map blends dense neon city blocks with sprawling beaches, wetlands and rural backroads.
Rockstar has teased a living economy where stores, businesses and people follow their own routines.
It is the first brand-new mainline GTA in over a decade, following GTA V's 2013 release.
Online multiplayer is planned to arrive after the single-player launch, not on day one.
